channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Production-Ready AI Agents: 5 Lessons from Refactoring a Monolith
item.description: The blog discusses how a sales research prototype was transformed into a reliable production AI agent using Google's Agent Development Kit, enhancing scalability and transparency. Key improvements included replacing monolithic scripts with sub-agents and implementing observability tools.

item.title: Tim Cook's Legacy Is Turning Apple Into a Subscription
item.description: As Tim Cook prepares to leave Apple, his focus on services has set the stage for incoming CEO John Ternus to lead the company into the AI era. This transition emphasizes the importance of adapting to new technologies.

item.title: More Cisco SD-WAN bugs battered in attacks
item.description: CISA has alerted federal agencies about three vulnerabilities in Cisco's SD-WAN Manager that are currently under attack, giving them four days to implement patches. This highlights ongoing cybersecurity challenges.

item.title: 5 Apple products explain my optimism for John Ternus as the next CEO
item.description: The article expresses optimism for John Ternus as Apple's next CEO, highlighting five products that showcase the company's potential under his leadership. It suggests a positive outlook for Apple's future innovations.

item.title: This Motorola phone deal comes with free Bluetooth trackers and earbuds - how it works
item.description: Motorola is promoting its latest Moto G phone by offering a deal that includes free Bluetooth trackers and earbuds. This marketing strategy aims to attract new customers.

item.title: Building agent-first governance and security
item.description: As AI agents become more prevalent in organizations, the article discusses the risks associated with insecure agents that could be exploited to access sensitive data. It emphasizes the need for governance and security measures.

item.title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
item.description: Clarifai has deleted 3 million photos provided by OkCupid for training facial recognition AI, following an FTC settlement. This action reflects ongoing scrutiny over data privacy practices.

item.title: Verizon will give you Meta Ray-Bans for free with this Fios Internet deal - how to get yours
item.description: Verizon is offering free Meta Ray-Ban glasses to customers who sign up for specific Fios Internet plans. This promotion aims to enhance customer acquisition.

item.title: End-to-end lineage with DVC and Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow apps
item.description: The article explains how to integrate DVC, Amazon SageMaker AI, and MLflow Apps to establish end-to-end machine learning model lineage, providing practical examples for implementation.

item.title: Phone-to-satellite use goes into orbit, growing 25% in 8 months
item.description: The direct-to-device satellite market has seen a 25% increase in connections over eight months, with the US and Starlink leading the growth. This indicates a rising trend in satellite communications.

item.title: Can a near infrared laptop light boost my mood? I tried it to find out
item.description: The SunBooster SLS2000 is a device that attaches to your laptop and mimics sunlight, claiming to enhance mood and focus. This product taps into the growing interest in light therapy for mental well-being.

item.title: This free app makes journaling so easy that I've managed to do it for 3 months
item.description: A new journaling app simplifies the process, making it easier for users to maintain a journaling habit for three months. This reflects a trend towards digital solutions for personal development.

item.title: CachyOS is the Arch Linux distro to try if you want serious speed and performance
item.description: CachyOS, a fast and performance-oriented Linux distribution, has released an updated version that enhances its capabilities. This positions it as a top choice for users seeking speed in their operating system.

item.title: CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data
item.description: CodeQL, GitHub's static analysis tool, now allows users to create custom sanitizers and validators, enhancing its ability to identify security issues in code. This update is significant for developers focused on security.

item.title: Modernizing the Facebook Groups Search to Unlock the Power of Community Knowledge
item.description: Facebook has revamped its Groups Search feature to improve the discovery and validation of community content. This update aims to enhance user experience and engagement within groups.

item.title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
item.description: Latitude has launched Voyage, a platform that enables users to create their own AI-powered role-playing games. This innovation could transform the gaming landscape by democratizing game development.

item.title: macOS ClickFix attacks deliver AppleScript stealers to snarf credentials, wallets
item.description: A ClickFix campaign has targeted macOS users with an AppleScript-based infostealer that collects sensitive data from browsers and cryptocurrency wallets. This highlights ongoing security threats to Apple users.

item.title: Anthropic bakes memory fixes into Bun 1.1.13 as developers complain of leaks
item.description: The latest update of the Bun JavaScript toolkit addresses critical memory leak issues that have been affecting performance in production environments. This is a significant improvement for developers using Bun.

item.title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
item.description: Bond, a new social media platform, aims to reduce doomscrolling by using AI to encourage users to engage in real-world activities. This reflects a growing concern about digital well-being.

item.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
item.description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection technology to help celebrities manage their digital presence by identifying and removing deepfakes. This move underscores the platform's commitment to protecting users.

item.title: The spaghettified DBMS chart that shows Oracle's crown is slowly slipping
item.description: Gartner's latest DBMS Market Share Ranks show that dominant database vendors, including Oracle, are gradually losing market share, indicating a shift in the industry landscape.

item.title: 3 new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
item.description: Google Ads Advisor is introducing three new features aimed at enhancing safety and speed for advertisers, improving the overall user experience.

item.title: Samsung is ending Messages in July: 5 replacements I'd switch to now
item.description: Samsung will discontinue its built-in messaging app in July, prompting users to seek alternative messaging solutions.

item.title: Ordering with the Starbucks ChatGPT app was a true coffee nightmare
item.description: A user experienced significant difficulties ordering coffee through the Starbucks ChatGPT app, highlighting potential flaws in AI-driven customer service.

item.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
item.description: OpenAI has launched Codex Labs to help enterprises implement Codex in software development, partnering with major firms like Accenture and PwC.

item.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
item.description: The article discusses the importance of openness in AI for enhancing cybersecurity, emphasizing collaborative efforts to combat threats.

item.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in APAC countries
item.description: Google has begun rolling out its Gemini feature in Chrome across several APAC countries, enhancing browser capabilities for users in those regions.

item.title: z3rno-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The z3rno-mcp has been added to PyPI, providing tools for memory operations in various MCP clients, enhancing their functionality.

item.title: z3rno-mcp 0.1.0
item.description: The z3rno-mcp 0.1.0 release provides new memory operation tools for Claude Desktop and other MCP clients, enhancing their capabilities.

item.title: 2026 Mercedes-Benz C-Class EV debuts – W520-gen with 800V tech, 330 kW DC, up to 800 km range WLTP
item.description: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz C-Class EV has been unveiled, featuring advanced technology and a competitive range, positioning it against rivals like the BMW i3.

item.title: Google Photos Can Now Smooth Skin and Whiten Teeth Via New ‘Touch-Up’ Menu
item.description: Google Photos has introduced a 'Touch-Up' menu that allows users to enhance their appearance by smoothing skin and whitening teeth in photos. This feature aims to improve user experience and engagement with the app.

item.title: coding-agent-wrapper added to PyPI
item.description: A new Python library called coding-agent-wrapper has been added to PyPI, enabling users to orchestrate coding agents like Claude Code and Codex with tool servers and credential management. This streamlines the development process for AI applications.

item.title: Own your digital vibe with Fix Your Search
item.description: Tonia Ryan has launched 'Fix Your Search' to help individuals manage their online presence and ensure their Google search results reflect their desired image. This initiative empowers users to take control of their digital reputation.

item.title: linkedin-network-cleaner added to PyPI
item.description: The linkedin-network-cleaner has been added to PyPI, allowing users to extract, score, and clean their LinkedIn networks using AI. This tool aims to enhance professional networking efficiency.

item.title: What to expect during the Phi Moments @ Next event: Join theCUBE April 22-23
item.description: The Phi Moments @ Next event will showcase advancements in AI by major providers like Google Cloud, focusing on how partners like Quantiphi Inc. utilize these technologies. This highlights the collaborative nature of AI development.

item.title: See video of Earthset that an Artemis II astronaut filmed on his iPhone while circling the moon
item.description: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman shared a video of Earthset filmed during the Artemis II mission while orbiting the moon. This footage offers a unique perspective of Earth from space.

item.title: Is XRP Gearing Up For A 35% Move? This Pattern May Suggest So
item.description: A crypto analyst suggests that XRP may be poised for a 35% price movement based on a symmetrical triangle pattern observed in its price chart. This analysis could influence trader decisions in the cryptocurrency market.

item.title: I solved my read-it-later bookmarks problem with a reading inbox in Obsidian
item.description: An individual shares their experience of organizing read-it-later bookmarks using a reading inbox in Obsidian, which helped them manage their reading materials more effectively. This approach encourages better information consumption.

item.title: Adobe deploys agents across its customer experience tools
item.description: Adobe has launched a new enterprise platform to integrate AI agents across its marketing and customer engagement tools, aiming to enhance customer experiences. This positions Adobe as a key player in AI-driven customer solutions.

item.title: Elon Musk skips probe as France investigates X
item.description: French prosecutors are investigating whether X's AI chatbot Grok contributed to the dissemination of harmful content, including child sexual abuse material and Holocaust denial. This raises serious ethical concerns about AI's role in content moderation.

item.title: Apple releases beta 3 for iPadOS 26.5, watchOS 26.5, and more
item.description: Apple has released developer beta 3 for iPadOS 26.5, watchOS 26.5, and tvOS 26.5, continuing its software development cycle. This update is significant for developers testing new features ahead of public releases.

item.title: valuehead added to PyPI
item.description: The ValueHead agent trace evaluation platform has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), providing a new SDK for developers. This addition facilitates easier integration and use of the platform in Python applications.

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT for the Best Alternatives To Investing in Real Estate — Here’s What It Said
item.description: An exploration of alternatives to real estate investing was conducted using ChatGPT, showcasing AI's capability to provide diverse investment options. This highlights the growing role of AI in financial decision-making.

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability (CVE-2026-5760) has been identified in SGLang, allowing remote code execution through malicious GGUF model files. With a CVSS score of 9.8, this poses a significant risk to affected systems.

item.title: Fitbit Air leak points to a May launch and plenty of color options
item.description: Google's Fitbit Air is set to launch in May, featuring a subscription-free design and various color options. This product aims to compete directly with Whoop in the fitness tracker market.

item.title: Elon Musk skips hearing in French probe of X
item.description: Elon Musk did not attend a French court hearing related to X and its AI chatbot Grok, which is under investigation for algorithmic abuse and fraudulent data extraction. This absence raises questions about accountability in tech leadership.

item.title: Mercedes-Benz Just Unveiled Its All-Electric C-Class—Here’s a First Look
item.description: Mercedes-Benz has unveiled its all-electric 2027 C-Class sedan, featuring a striking design and advanced technology with a 350-mile range. This launch emphasizes the automaker's commitment to electric vehicles.

item.title: Conductor Launches AgentStack for Answer Engine Optimization
item.description: Conductor has launched AgentStack, an enterprise suite designed for optimizing AI search visibility through agentic LLM applications and developer tools. This aims to enhance the efficiency of AI-driven search engines.

item.title: ramjetio 0.7.3
item.description: The ramjetio 0.7.3 update introduces a distributed cache system for PyTorch training, enhancing performance and efficiency for machine learning tasks. This update is beneficial for developers working with large datasets.

item.title: AI Chatbots Often Push Risky Cancer Treatment Alternatives
item.description: A study published in BMJ Open found that AI chatbots often suggest risky cancer treatment alternatives, raising concerns about the reliability of AI in healthcare. This underscores the need for careful oversight in AI medical advice.
